articles.headline: Meta Announces $9.1 Billion AI Data Center in Canada, Its Largest Outside the U.S.
articles.summary: Meta plans to invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first AI data center in Canada, marking a major expansion of AI infrastructure outside the U.S. and potentially reshaping regional cloud capacity and energy demands.

articles.headline: IBM Sparks Market Rout as Businesses Shift Spending from Software to AI Data-Center Infrastructure
articles.summary: IBM forecasted second-quarter revenue below estimates, signaling a major shift where corporations are prioritizing AI data-center infrastructure over traditional software spending, a trend that could pressure the broader enterprise software sector.

articles.headline: Apple Raises MacBook and iPad Prices Due to AI-Driven Memory and Storage Chip Costs
articles.summary: Apple hiked prices on popular products like the MacBook and iPad, directly attributing the increase to rising costs for memory and storage chips driven by the AI infrastructure boom, which may ripple through consumer and business hardware pricing.
